summary: The court's clerk issued an administrative notice directing plaintiff Austin Beaulier to file a Report on the Filing or Determination of an Action Regarding a Copyright (Form AO 121) within five days, as required by 17 U. S. C. § 508, in connection with the complaint filed on March 27, 2026. The entry also notes an electronic filing error and a modification made on March 31, 2026.

The court's clerk scheduled an Initial Case Management Conference for July 17, 2026, at 8:30 AM via Zoom, with a Joint Case Management Statement due from both parties by July 10, 2026. Counsel wishing to appear or argue must submit their names and emails to the court by July 14, 2026, at 3:00 PM PST.

The court reassigned this case to Judge Charles R. Breyer through a random selection process, removing Magistrate Judge Sallie Kim from the case. All previously scheduled hearing and trial dates are vacated, but existing briefing schedules and other deadlines—such as those for ADR compliance and discovery—remain in effect; counsel must refile hearing notices before Judge Breyer.

The Clerk of Court issued a notice that this case will be randomly reassigned from a Magistrate Judge to a District Court Judge, because at least one party has not consented to Magistrate Judge jurisdiction. All hearings currently scheduled before the Magistrate Judge are vacated and must be rescheduled before the newly assigned District Judge.
Plaintiff Austin Beaulier filed a form indicating whether he consents to or declines having a U. S. Magistrate Judge preside over the case, a standard early-stage procedural step in federal court.
The magistrate judge granted a motion allowing attorney Michael A. Acciavatti to appear in this case despite not being a member of the Northern District of California bar.

A filing fee of $328 was paid along with the motion.
